reflexion_entries_to_archive: S122-P1 #cross-check-oracle-catches-your-own-layer: the mirror-vs-worker hashWorldState oracle's first live catch was MY OWN prediction layer (drag-preserve restore ran before the hash -> 6 mismatches), not the apply path. Order integrity checks to validate EXACTLY the layer under test (apply), before deliberate divergences (prediction/UX) re-enter. An oracle that fires on designed divergence trains people to ignore it.

S122-P1 #measure-first-changed-the-design: the pre-registered ROI rule v2 FAILED on the TD-heavy re-measure (structuredClone p95 10.1ms @6x — 5x the stringify cost, refuting the 'simple clone' default BOTH Council seats and I expected to pass) -> the positions-buffer + structural-snapshot format came FROM the measurement, not the deliberation. Pre-registering the rule + the contingency ladder BEFORE measuring made the escalation mechanical instead of a debate.

S122-P2 #suppress-competing-recovery-paths: the S82 auto-reconnect (transport teardown+rejoin @1s) and the D3 migration grace COMPETE for the same transport — a survivor mid-reconnect-cycle misses the claim broadcast. Seam-gated suppression + idempotent re-broadcast x5 made the e2e deterministic. When two recovery protocols share a resource, serialize them explicitly; do not let timers interleave.

S122 #both-deploy-paths-ran: with Actions back alive, the master-push auto-deploy AND the manual gh-pages force-push both deployed the same session — they converged harmlessly this time, but dual-writer deploy paths are a race waiting for a divergence. Surface the owner decision at every close until one path is retired.

S122 #feed-platform-constraints-into-council-prompts: BOTH Council seats bet R1 architecture on SharedArrayBuffer without knowing the deploy target (GH Pages) cannot set COOP/COEP headers. A 5-line PLATFORM CONSTRAINTS block in the R1 prompt (hosting, headers, worker/SAB availability, bundle charter) would have redirected both seats' effort to viable alternatives. Adopted for the S123 PDR template.
